# User Story Mapping Template

## Purpose
Map user journeys to activities and stories to plan releases, validate user value, and sequence
work effectively.

## When to Use
- Use for release planning and prioritization across multiple stories and activities.
- Use to connect personas, journeys, and dependencies at a product level.
- Use alongside (not instead of) the user story template for story-level detail.

## Audience
Product Owner, UX, delivery team, and stakeholders.

## Inputs
- Product vision and personas.
- Journey research and usage analytics.
- Backlog themes and release timelines.

## 1. Executive Summary
Provide a short overview of the product/feature, target users, and intended outcomes.

## 2. User Story Mapping Overview
- **Product/Feature:**
- **Target Users:**
- **Release Timeline:**
- **Team:**

## 3. User Journey Backbone
### High-Level User Activities
```
[User Goal 1] → [User Goal 2] → [User Goal 3] → [User Goal 4]
     |             |             |             |
[Detailed        [Detailed     [Detailed     [Detailed
 Stories]         Stories]      Stories]      Stories]
```

### User Activities Definition
| Activity | User Goal | Success Criteria |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |

## 9. Definition of Done
### Story Level
- [ ] Acceptance criteria met
- [ ] Code reviewed and approved
- [ ] Unit tests written and passing
- [ ] Integration tests passing
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] UX/UI review completed

### Release Level
- [ ] All stories in release completed
- [ ] End-to-end testing completed
- [ ] Performance testing passed
- [ ] Security review completed
- [ ] Deployment plan executed
- [ ] User training materials ready

## 10. Measurement & Learning
### Success Metrics
- **User Engagement:**
- **Feature Adoption:**
- **User Satisfaction:**
- **Business Impact:**

### Learning Plan
- [ ] User feedback collection method
- [ ] Analytics implementation
- [ ] A/B testing strategy
- [ ] Regular retrospectives
